content: By integrating multiple inconsistent reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanism research into the Self-Refine framework's iterative self-feedback loop, we can create a multi-perspective self-refinement system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no additional training or supervision while resolving the blind spot bias that single-perspective self-feedback cannot avoid. This hybrid system lets an LLM critique its own output from conflicting problem-solving frames of reference iteratively, leading to greater output improvements than either approach alone.
